content.problemStatement:
Q7) A. Explain any two techniques used for handling missing data in a dataset. How do they impact
model performance?  ---4 Marks
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a short description of a dataset (at most 100 characters). The description line is read and ignored.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print exactly two lines in the following order:
1. "Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data."
2. "They impact model performance by reducing bias and improving accuracy."
The two lines are printed consecutively for every test case, preserving the order of the test cases.

sampleTestCases.output: Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.\nThey impact model performance by reducing bias and improving accuracy.\n
sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case; the program prints the two required sentences once.

sampleTestCases.output:
Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.
They impact model performance by reducing bias and improving accuracy.
Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.
They impact model performance by reducing bias and improving accuracy.
sampleTestCases.explanation: Two test cases lead to the two sentences being printed twice, in order.

sampleTestCases.output:
Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.
They impact model performance by reducing bias and improving accuracy.
Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.
They impact model performance by reducing bias and improving accuracy.
Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.
They impact model performance by reducing bias and improving accuracy.
sampleTestCases.explanation: Three test cases produce three repetitions of the two‑line block.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times, each time read (and discard) the description line. After discarding, output the two fixed sentences exactly as required. No computation beyond I/O is needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored regardless of T.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
if not lines:
    sys.exit()
T = int(lines[0].strip())
out = []
for i in range(1, T + 1):
    # description line may be present at lines[i]; we ignore it
    out.append("Mean imputation and regression imputation are two techniques used for handling missing data.")
    out.append("They impact model performance by reducing bias and improving accuracy.")
sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out))
